file_insights.description: Added `print_cleanup_summary()` function to report cleanup operation results
key_features: Calculates total_cards from three removal categories
Dual-mode output: dry-run vs actual execution

patterns_discovered.description: Distinct output paths for dry-run vs actual execution modes
Ternary operator in println! for singular/plural 'director[y/ies]'
Division by 1_048_576 constant for MB formatting with 2-decimal precision
patterns_discovered.consistency_note: magic number not extracted to constant

patterns_discovered.description: ASCII-art style comment header (dashes) before function group

gotchas_discovered.description: 1_048_576 hardcoded instead of named constant; makes maintenance harder if conversion factor needs adjustment
Inline ternary operators in println! calls make code less testable; should extract to helper function

gotchas_discovered.issue: function assumes stats aggregation already done
gotchas_discovered.description: No validation that CleanupStats::bytes_freed is reasonable; trusts caller

approach_details.execution: Clean implementation with clear dry-run/actual branching and human-readable output
approach_details.alignment: Follows existing code style (section comments, println patterns)
approach_details.completeness: Function covers all required metrics (card count, target dirs removed, MB freed)

recommendations.suggestion: Extract byte-to-MB conversion to named constant: `const MB_BYTES: f64 = 1_048_576.0;`
recommendations.benefit: Single source of truth for unit conversion

recommendations.suggestion: Extract pluralization to helper: `fn pluralize(count: usize, singular: &str, plural: &str) -> String`
Add unit tests with edge cases: zero cards, single card, large byte counts
Add doc comment explaining stats parameter and output format
recommendations.benefit: Improve IDE hints and API clarity
